verses.sanskrit: सूत उवाचविदुरस्तीर्थयात्रायां मैत्रेयादात्मनो गतिम् ।ज्ञात्वागाद्धास्तिनपुरं तयावाप्तविवित्सित: ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: sūta uvācaviduras tīrtha-yātrāyāṁmaitreyād ātmano gatimjñātvāgād dhāstinapuraṁtayāvāpta-vivitsitaḥ
verses.text: Śrī Sūta Gosvāmī said: While traveling on a pilgrimage, Vidura received knowledge of the destination of the self from the great sage Maitreya and then returned to Hastināpura. He became as well versed in the subject as he desired.

verses.sanskrit: यावत: कृतवान् प्रश्नान् क्षत्ता कौषारवाग्रत: ।जातैकभक्तिर्गोविन्दे तेभ्यश्चोपरराम ह ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: yāvataḥ kṛtavān praśnānkṣattā kauṣāravāgrataḥjātaika-bhaktir govindetebhyaś copararāma ha
verses.text: After asking various questions and becoming established in the transcendental loving service of Lord Kṛṣṇa, Vidura retired from putting questions to Maitreya Muni.

verses.sanskrit: तं बन्धुमागतं द‍ृष्ट्वा धर्मपुत्र: सहानुज: ।धृतराष्ट्रो युयुत्सुश्च सूत: शारद्वत: पृथा ॥ ३ ॥गान्धारी द्रौपदी ब्रह्मन् सुभद्रा चोत्तरा कृपी ।अन्याश्च जामय: पाण्डोर्ज्ञातय: ससुता: स्त्रिय: ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: taṁ bandhum āgataṁ dṛṣṭvādharma-putraḥ sahānujaḥdhṛtarāṣṭro yuyutsuś casūtaḥ śāradvataḥ pṛthā
verses.text: When they saw Vidura return to the palace, all the inhabitants — Mahārāja Yudhiṣṭhira, his younger brothers, Dhṛtarāṣṭra, Sātyaki, Sañjaya, Kṛpācārya, Kuntī, Gāndhārī, Draupadī, Subhadrā, Uttarā, Kṛpī, many other wives of the Kauravas, and other ladies with children — all hurried to him in great delight. It so appeared that they had regained their consciousness after a long period.

verses.sanskrit: प्रत्युज्जग्मु: प्रहर्षेण प्राणं तन्व इवागतम् ।अभिसङ्गम्य विधिवत् परिष्वङ्गाभिवादनै: ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: pratyujjagmuḥ praharṣeṇaprāṇaṁ tanva ivāgatamabhisaṅgamya vidhivatpariṣvaṅgābhivādanaiḥ
verses.text: With great delight they all approached him, as if life had returned to their bodies. They exchanged obeisances and welcomed each other with embraces.

verses.sanskrit: मुमुचु: प्रेमबाष्पौघं विरहौत्कण्ठ्यकातरा: ।राजा तमर्हयाञ्चक्रे कृतासनपरिग्रहम् ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: mumucuḥ prema-bāṣpaughaṁvirahautkaṇṭhya-kātarāḥrājā tam arhayāṁ cakrekṛtāsana-parigraham
verses.text: Due to anxieties and long separation, they all cried out of affection. King Yudhiṣṭhira then arranged to offer sitting accommodations and a reception.

verses.sanskrit: तं भुक्तवन्तं विश्रान्तमासीनं सुखमासने ।प्रश्रयावनतो राजा प्राह तेषां च श‍ृण्वताम् ॥ ७ ॥
verses.transliteration: taṁ bhuktavantaṁ viśrāntamāsīnaṁ sukham āsanepraśrayāvanato rājāprāha teṣāṁ ca śṛṇvatām
verses.text: After Vidura ate sumptuously and took sufficient rest, he was comfortably seated. Then the King began to speak to him, and all who were present there listened.

verses.sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाचअपि स्मरथ नो युष्मत्पक्षच्छायासमेधितान् ।विपद्गणाद्विषाग्‍न्‍यादेर्मोचिता यत्समातृका: ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvācaapi smaratha no yuṣmat-pakṣa-cchāyā-samedhitānvipad-gaṇād viṣāgnyādermocitā yat samātṛkāḥ
verses.text: Mahārāja Yudhiṣṭhira said: My uncle, do you remember how you always protected us, along with our mother, from all sorts of calamities? Your partiality, like the wings of a bird, saved us from poisoning and arson.

verses.sanskrit: कया वृत्त्या वर्तितं वश्चरद्भ‍ि: क्षितिमण्डलम् ।तीर्थानि क्षेत्रमुख्यानि सेवितानीह भूतले ॥ ९ ॥
verses.transliteration: kayā vṛttyā vartitaṁ vaścaradbhiḥ kṣiti-maṇḍalamtīrthāni kṣetra-mukhyānisevitānīha bhūtale
verses.text: While traveling on the surface of the earth, how did you maintain your livelihood? At which holy places and pilgrimage sites did you render service?

verses.sanskrit: भवद्विधा भागवतास्तीर्थभूता: स्वयं विभो ।तीर्थीकुर्वन्ति तीर्थानि स्वान्त:स्थेन गदाभृता ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: bhavad-vidhā bhāgavatāstīrtha-bhūtāḥ svayaṁ vibhotīrthī-kurvanti tīrthānisvāntaḥ-sthena gadābhṛtā
verses.text: My lord, devotees like your good self are verily holy places personified. Because you carry the Personality of Godhead within your heart, you turn all places into places of pilgrimage.

verses.sanskrit: अपि न: सुहृदस्तात बान्धवा: कृष्णदेवता: ।द‍ृष्टा: श्रुता वा यदव: स्वपुर्यां सुखमासते ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: api naḥ suhṛdas tātabāndhavāḥ kṛṣṇa-devatāḥdṛṣṭāḥ śrutā vā yadavaḥsva-puryāṁ sukham āsate
verses.text: My uncle, you must have visited Dvārakā. In that holy place are our friends and well-wishers, the descendants of Yadu, who are always rapt in the service of the Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa. You might have seen them or heard about them. Are they all living happily in their abodes?

verses.sanskrit: इत्युक्तो धर्मराजेन सर्वं तत् समवर्णयत् ।यथानुभूतंक्रमशो विना यदुकुलक्षयम् ॥ १२ ॥
verses.transliteration: ity ukto dharma-rājenasarvaṁ tat samavarṇayatyathānubhūtaṁ kramaśovinā yadu-kula-kṣayam
verses.text: Thus being questioned by Mahārāja Yudhiṣṭhira, Mahātmā Vidura gradually described everything he had personally experienced, except news of the annihilation of the Yadu dynasty.

verses.sanskrit: नन्वप्रियं दुर्विषहं नृणांस्वयमुपस्थितम् ।नावेदयत् सकरुणो दु:खितान् द्रष्टुमक्षम: ॥ १३ ॥
verses.transliteration: nanv apriyaṁ durviṣahaṁnṛṇāṁ svayam upasthitamnāvedayat sakaruṇoduḥkhitān draṣṭum akṣamaḥ
verses.text: Compassionate Mahātmā Vidura could not stand to see the Pāṇḍavas distressed at any time. Therefore he did not disclose this unpalatable and unbearable incident because calamities come of their own accord.

verses.sanskrit: कञ्चित्कालमथावात्सीत्सत्कृतो देववत्सुखम् ।भ्रातुर्ज्येष्ठस्य श्रेयस्कृत्सर्वेषां सुखमावहन् ॥ १४ ॥
verses.transliteration: kañcit kālam athāvātsītsat-kṛto devavat sukhambhrātur jyeṣṭhasya śreyas-kṛtsarveṣāṁ sukham āvahan
verses.text: Thus Mahātmā Vidura, being treated just like a godly person by his kinsmen, remained there for a certain period just to rectify the mentality of his eldest brother and in this way bring happiness to all the others.

verses.sanskrit: अबिभ्रदर्यमा दण्डं यथावदघकारिषु ।यावद्दधार शूद्रत्वं शापाद्वर्षशतं यम: ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: abibhrad aryamā daṇḍaṁyathāvad agha-kāriṣuyāvad dadhāra śūdratvaṁśāpād varṣa-śataṁ yamaḥ
verses.text: As long as Vidura played the part of a śūdra, being cursed by Maṇḍūka Muni, Aryamā officiated at the post of Yamarāja to punish those who committed sinful acts.

verses.sanskrit: युधिष्ठिरो लब्धराज्यो द‍ृष्ट्वा पौत्रं कुलन्धरम् ।भ्रातृभिर्लोकपालाभैर्मुमुदे परया श्रिया ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: yudhiṣṭhiro labdha-rājyodṛṣṭvā pautraṁ kulan-dharambhrātṛbhir loka-pālābhairmumude parayā śriyā
verses.text: Having won his kingdom and observed the birth of one grandson competent to continue the noble tradition of his family, Mahārāja Yudhiṣṭhira reigned peacefully and enjoyed uncommon opulence in cooperation with his younger brothers, who were all expert administrators to the common people.

verses.sanskrit: एवं गृहेषु सक्तानां प्रमत्तानां तदीहया ।अत्यक्रामदविज्ञात: काल: परमदुस्तर: ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: evaṁ gṛheṣu saktānāṁpramattānāṁ tad-īhayāatyakrāmad avijñātaḥkālaḥ parama-dustaraḥ
verses.text: Insurmountable eternal time imperceptibly overcomes those who are too much attached to family affairs and are always engrossed in their thought.

verses.sanskrit: विदुरस्तदभिप्रेत्य धृतराष्ट्रमभाषत ।राजन्निर्गम्यतां शीघ्रं पश्येदं भयमागतम् ॥ १८ ॥
verses.transliteration: viduras tad abhipretyadhṛtarāṣṭram abhāṣatarājan nirgamyatāṁ śīghraṁpaśyedaṁ bhayam āgatam
verses.text: Mahātmā Vidura knew all this, and therefore he addressed Dhṛtarāṣṭra, saying: My dear King, please get out of here immediately. Do not delay. Just see how fear has overtaken you.

verses.sanskrit: प्रतिक्रिया न यस्येह कुतश्चित्कर्हिचित्प्रभो ।स एष भगवान् काल: सर्वेषां न: समागत: ॥ १९ ॥
verses.transliteration: pratikriyā na yasyehakutaścit karhicit prabhosa eṣa bhagavān kālaḥsarveṣāṁ naḥ samāgataḥ
verses.text: This frightful situation cannot be remedied by any person in this material world. My lord, it is the Supreme Personality of Godhead as eternal time [kāla] that has approached us all.

verses.sanskrit: येन चैवाभिपन्नोऽयं प्राणै: प्रियतमैरपि ।जन: सद्यो वियुज्येत किमुतान्यैर्धनादिभि: ॥ २० ॥
verses.transliteration: yena caivābhipanno ’yaṁprāṇaiḥ priyatamair apijanaḥ sadyo viyujyetakim utānyair dhanādibhiḥ
verses.text: Whoever is under the influence of supreme kāla [eternal time] must surrender his most dear life, and what to speak of other things, such as wealth, honor, children, land and home.

verses.sanskrit: पितृभ्रातृसुहृत्पुत्रा हतास्ते विगतं वयम् ।आत्मा च जरया ग्रस्त: परगेहमुपाससे ॥ २१ ॥
verses.transliteration: pitṛ-bhrātṛ-suhṛt-putrāhatās te vigataṁ vayamātmā ca jarayā grastaḥpara-geham upāsase
verses.text: Your father, brother, well-wishers and sons are all dead and passed away. You yourself have expended the major portion of your life, your body is now overtaken by invalidity, and you are living in the home of another.

verses.sanskrit: अन्ध: पुरैव वधिरो मन्दप्रज्ञाश्च साम्प्रतम् ।विशीर्णदन्तो मन्दाग्नि: सराग: कफमुद्वहन् ॥ २२ ॥
verses.transliteration: andhaḥ puraiva vadhiromanda-prajñāś ca sāmprataṁviśīrṇa-danto mandāgniḥsarāgaḥ kapham udvahan
verses.text: You have been blind from your very birth, and recently you have become hard of hearing. Your memory is shortened, and your intelligence is disturbed. Your teeth are loose, your liver is defective, and you are coughing up mucus.

verses.sanskrit: अहो महीयसी जन्तोर्जीविताशा यथा भवान् ।भीमापवर्जितंपिण्डमादत्ते गृहपालवत् ॥ २३ ॥
verses.transliteration: aho mahīyasī jantorjīvitāśā yathā bhavānbhīmāpavarjitaṁ piṇḍamādatte gṛha-pālavat
verses.text: Alas, how powerful are the hopes of a living being to continue his life. Verily, you are living just like a household dog and are eating remnants of food given by Bhīma.

verses.sanskrit: अग्निर्निसृष्टो दत्तश्च गरो दाराश्चदूषिता: ।हृतं क्षेत्रं धनं येषां तद्दत्तैरसुभि: कियत् ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: agnir nisṛṣṭo dattaś cagaro dārāś ca dūṣitāḥhṛtaṁ kṣetraṁ dhanaṁ yeṣāṁtad-dattair asubhiḥ kiyat
verses.text: There is no need to live a degraded life and subsist on the charity of those whom you tried to kill by arson and poisoning. You also insulted their married wife and usurped their kingdom and wealth.

verses.sanskrit: तस्यापि तव देहोऽयं कृपणस्य जिजीविषो: ।परैत्यनिच्छतो जीर्णो जरया वाससी इव ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasyāpi tava deho ’yaṁkṛpaṇasya jijīviṣoḥparaity anicchato jīrṇojarayā vāsasī iva
verses.text: Despite your unwillingness to die and your desire to live even at the cost of honor and prestige, your miserly body will certainly dwindle and deteriorate like an old garment.

verses.sanskrit: गतस्वार्थमिमं देहं विरक्तो मुक्तबन्धन: ।अविज्ञातगतिर्जह्यात् स वै धीर उदाहृत: ॥ २६ ॥
verses.transliteration: gata-svārtham imaṁ dehaṁvirakto mukta-bandhanaḥavijñāta-gatir jahyātsa vai dhīra udāhṛtaḥ
verses.text: He is called undisturbed who goes to an unknown, remote place and, freed from all obligations, quits his material body when it has become useless.

verses.sanskrit: य: स्वकात्परतो वेह जातनिर्वेद आत्मवान् ।हृदि कृत्वा हरिं गेहात्प्रव्रजेत्स नरोत्तम: ॥ २७ ॥
verses.transliteration: yaḥ svakāt parato vehajāta-nirveda ātmavānhṛdi kṛtvā hariṁ gehātpravrajet sa narottamaḥ
verses.text: He is certainly a first-class man who awakens and understands, either by himself or from others, the falsity and misery of this material world and thus leaves home and depends fully on the Personality of Godhead residing within his heart.

verses.sanskrit: अथोदीचीं दिशं यातु स्वैरज्ञातगतिर्भवान् ।इतोऽर्वाक्प्रायश: काल: पुंसां गुणविकर्षण: ॥ २८ ॥
verses.transliteration: athodīcīṁ diśaṁ yātusvair ajñāta-gatir bhavānito ’rvāk prāyaśaḥ kālaḥpuṁsāṁ guṇa-vikarṣaṇaḥ
verses.text: Please, therefore, leave for the North immediately, without letting your relatives know, for soon that time will approach which will diminish the good qualities of men.

verses.sanskrit: एवं राजा विदुरेणानुजेनप्रज्ञाचक्षुर्बोधित आजमीढ: ।छित्त्वा स्वेषु स्‍नेहपाशान्द्रढिम्नोनिश्चक्राम भ्रातृसन्दर्शिताध्वा ॥ २९ ॥
verses.transliteration: evaṁ rājā vidureṇānujenaprajñā-cakṣur bodhita ājamīḍhaḥchittvā sveṣu sneha-pāśān draḍhimnoniścakrāma bhrātṛ-sandarśitādhvā
verses.text: Thus Mahārāja Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the scion of the family of Ajamīḍha, firmly convinced by introspective knowledge [prajñā], broke at once the strong network of familial affection by his resolute determination. Thus he immediately left home to set out on the path of liberation, as directed by his younger brother Vidura.

verses.sanskrit: पतिं प्रयान्तं सुबलस्य पुत्रीपतिव्रता चानुजगाम साध्वी ।हिमालयं न्यस्तदण्डप्रहर्षंमनस्विनामिव सत्सम्प्रहार: ॥ ३० ॥
verses.transliteration: patiṁ prayāntaṁ subalasya putrīpati-vratā cānujagāma sādhvīhimālayaṁ nyasta-daṇḍa-praharṣaṁmanasvinām iva sat samprahāraḥ
verses.text: The gentle and chaste Gāndhārī, who was the daughter of King Subala of Kandahar [or Gāndhāra], followed her husband, seeing that he was going to the Himālaya Mountains, which are the delight of those who have accepted the staff of the renounced order like fighters who have accepted a good lashing from the enemy.

verses.sanskrit: अजातशत्रु: कृतमैत्रो हुताग्नि-र्विप्रान्नत्वातिलगोभूमिरुक्‍मै: ।गृहं प्रविष्टो गुरुवन्दनायन चापश्यत्पितरौसौबलीं च ॥ ३१ ॥
verses.transliteration: ajāta-śatruḥ kṛta-maitro hutāgnirviprān natvā tila-go-bhūmi-rukmaiḥgṛhaṁ praviṣṭo guru-vandanāyana cāpaśyat pitarau saubalīṁ ca
verses.text: Mahārāja Yudhiṣṭhira, whose enemy was never born, performed his daily morning duties by praying, offering fire sacrifice to the sun-god, and offering obeisances, grains, cows, land and gold to the brāhmaṇas. He then entered the palace to pay respects to the elderly. However, he could not find his uncles or aunt, the daughter of King Subala.

verses.sanskrit: तत्र सञ्जयमासीनं पप्रच्छोद्विग्नमानस: ।गावल्गणे क्‍व नस्तातो वृद्धो हीनश्च नेत्रयो: ॥ ३२ ॥
verses.transliteration: tatra sañjayam āsīnaṁpapracchodvigna-mānasaḥgāvalgaṇe kva nas tātovṛddho hīnaś ca netrayoḥ
verses.text: Mahārāja Yudhiṣṭhira, full of anxiety, turned to Sañjaya, who was sitting there, and said: O Sañjaya, where is our uncle, who is old and blind?

verses.sanskrit: अम्बा च हतपुत्रार्ता पितृव्य: क्‍व गत: सुहृत् ।अपि मय्यकृतप्रज्ञे हतबन्धु: स भार्यया ।आशंसमान: शमलं गङ्गायां दु:खितोऽपतत् ॥ ३३ ॥
verses.transliteration: ambā ca hata-putrārtāpitṛvyaḥ kva gataḥ suhṛtapi mayy akṛta-prajñehata-bandhuḥ sa bhāryayāāśaṁsamānaḥ śamalaṁgaṅgāyāṁ duḥkhito ’patat
verses.text: Where is my well-wisher, uncle Vidura, and mother Gāndhārī, who is very afflicted due to all her sons’ demise? My uncle Dhṛtarāṣṭra was also very mortified due to the death of all his sons and grandsons. Undoubtedly I am very ungrateful. Did he, therefore, take my offenses very seriously and, along with his wife, drown himself in the Ganges?

verses.sanskrit: पितर्युपरते पाण्डौ सर्वान्न: सुहृद: शिशून् ।अरक्षतां व्यसनत: पितृव्यौ क्‍व गतावित: ॥ ३४ ॥
verses.transliteration: pitary uparate pāṇḍausarvān naḥ suhṛdaḥ śiśūnarakṣatāṁ vyasanataḥpitṛvyau kva gatāv itaḥ
verses.text: When my father, Pāṇḍu, fell down and we were all small children, these two uncles gave us protection from all kinds of calamities. They were always our good well-wishers. Alas, where have they gone from here?

verses.sanskrit: सूत उवाचकृपया स्नेडहवैक्लव्यात्सूतो विरहकर्शित: ।आत्मेश्वरमचक्षाणो न प्रत्याहातिपीडित: ॥ ३५ ॥
verses.transliteration: sūta uvācakṛpayā sneha-vaiklavyātsūto viraha-karśitaḥātmeśvaram acakṣāṇona pratyāhātipīḍitaḥ
verses.text: Sūta Gosvāmī said: Because of compassion and mental agitation, Sañjaya, not having seen his own master, Dhṛtarāṣṭra, was aggrieved and could not properly reply to Mahārāja Yudhiṣṭhira.

verses.sanskrit: विमृज्याश्रूणि पाणिभ्यांविष्टभ्यात्मानमात्मना ।अजातशत्रुं प्रत्यूचे प्रभो: पादावनुस्मरन् ॥ ३६ ॥
verses.transliteration: vimṛjyāśrūṇi pāṇibhyāṁviṣṭabhyātmānam ātmanāajāta-śatruṁ pratyūceprabhoḥ pādāv anusmaran
verses.text: First he slowly pacified his mind by intelligence, and wiping away his tears and thinking of the feet of his master, Dhṛtarāṣṭra, he began to reply to Mahārāja Yudhiṣṭhira.

verses.sanskrit: सञ्जय उवाचनाहं वेद व्यवसितं पित्रोर्व: कुलनन्दन ।गान्धार्यावा महाबाहो मुषितोऽस्मि महात्मभि: ॥ ३७ ॥
verses.transliteration: sañjaya uvācanāhaṁ veda vyavasitaṁpitror vaḥ kula-nandanagāndhāryā vā mahā-bāhomuṣito ’smi mahātmabhiḥ
verses.text: Sañjaya said: My dear descendant of the Kuru dynasty, I have no information of the determination of your two uncles and Gāndhārī. O King, I have been cheated by those great souls.

verses.sanskrit: अथाजगाम भगवान् नारद: सहतुम्बुरु: ।प्रत्युत्थायाभिवाद्याह सानुजोऽभ्यर्चयन्मुनिम् ॥ ३८ ॥
verses.transliteration: athājagāma bhagavānnāradaḥ saha-tumburuḥpratyutthāyābhivādyāhasānujo ’bhyarcayan munim
verses.text: While Sañjaya was thus speaking, Śrī Nārada, the powerful devotee of the Lord, appeared on the scene carrying his tumburu. Mahārāja Yudhiṣṭhira and his brothers received him properly by getting up from their seats and offering obeisances.

verses.sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाचनाहं वेद गतिं पित्रोर्भगवन् क्‍वगतावित: ।अम्बा वा हतपुत्रार्ता क्‍व गता च तपस्विनी ॥ ३९ ॥
verses.transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvācanāhaṁ veda gatiṁ pitrorbhagavan kva gatāv itaḥambā vā hata-putrārtākva gatā ca tapasvinī
verses.text: Mahārāja Yudhiṣṭhira said: O godly personality, I do not know where my two uncles have gone. Nor can I find my ascetic aunt who is griefstricken by the loss of all her sons.

verses.sanskrit: कर्णधार इवापारे भगवान् पारदर्शक: ।अथाबभाषेभगवान् नारदो मुनिसत्तम: ॥ ४० ॥
verses.transliteration: karṇadhāra ivāpārebhagavān pāra-darśakaḥathābabhāṣe bhagavānnārado muni-sattamaḥ
verses.text: You are like a captain of a ship in a great ocean and you can direct us to our destination. Thus addressed, the godly personality, Devarṣi Nārada, greatest of the philosopher devotees, began to speak.

verses.sanskrit: नारद उवाचमा कञ्चन शुचो राजन् यदीश्वरवशं जगत् ।लोका: सपाला यस्येमे वहन्ति बलिमीशितु: ।स संयुनक्ति भूतानि स एव वियुनक्ति च ॥ ४१ ॥
verses.transliteration: nārada uvācamā kañcana śuco rājanyad īśvara-vaśaṁ jagatlokāḥ sapālā yasyemevahanti balim īśituḥsa saṁyunakti bhūtānisa eva viyunakti ca
verses.text: Śrī Nārada said: O pious King, do not lament for anyone, for everyone is under the control of the Supreme Lord. Therefore all living beings and their leaders carry on worship to be well protected. It is He only who brings them together and disperses them.

verses.sanskrit: यथा गावो नसि प्रोतास्तन्त्यां बद्धाश्च दामभि: ।वाक्तन्त्यां नामभिर्बद्धा वहन्ति बलिमीशितु: ॥ ४२ ॥
verses.transliteration: yathā gāvo nasi protāstantyāṁ baddhāś ca dāmabhiḥvāk-tantyāṁ nāmabhir baddhāvahanti balim īśituḥ
verses.text: As a cow, bound through the nose by a long rope, is conditioned, so also human beings are bound by different Vedic injunctions and are conditioned to obey the orders of the Supreme.

verses.sanskrit: यथा क्रीडोपस्कराणां संयोगविगमाविह ।इच्छया क्रीडितु: स्यातां तथैवेशेच्छया नृणाम् ॥ ४३ ॥
verses.transliteration: yathā krīḍopaskarāṇāṁsaṁyoga-vigamāv ihaicchayā krīḍituḥ syātāṁtathaiveśecchayā nṛṇām
verses.text: As a player sets up and disperses his playthings according to his own sweet will, so the supreme will of the Lord brings men together and separates them.

verses.sanskrit: यन्मन्यसे ध्रुवं लोकमध्रुवं वा न चोभयम् ।सर्वथा न हि शोच्यास्ते स्‍नेहादन्यत्र मोहजात् ॥ ४४ ॥
verses.transliteration: yan manyase dhruvaṁ lokamadhruvaṁ vā na cobhayamsarvathā na hi śocyās tesnehād anyatra mohajāt
verses.text: O King, in all circumstances, whether you consider the soul to be an eternal principle, or the material body to be perishable, or everything to exist in the impersonal Absolute Truth, or everything to be an inexplicable combination of matter and spirit, feelings of separation are due only to illusory affection and nothing more.

verses.sanskrit: तस्माज्जह्यङ्ग वैक्लव्यमज्ञानकृतमात्मन: ।कथं त्वनाथा: कृपणा वर्तेरंस्ते च मां विना ॥ ४५ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasmāj jahy aṅga vaiklavyamajñāna-kṛtam ātmanaḥkathaṁ tv anāthāḥ kṛpaṇāvarteraṁs te ca māṁ vinā
verses.text: Therefore give up your anxiety due to ignorance of the self. You are now thinking of how they, who are helpless poor creatures, will exist without you.

verses.sanskrit: कालकर्मगुणाधीनो देहोऽयं पाञ्चभौतिक: ।कथमन्यांस्तु गोपायेत्सर्पग्रस्तो यथा परम् ॥ ४६ ॥
verses.transliteration: kāla-karma-guṇādhīnodeho ’yaṁ pāñca-bhautikaḥkatham anyāṁs tu gopāyetsarpa-grasto yathā param
verses.text: This gross material body made of five elements is already under the control of eternal time [kāla], action [karma] and the modes of material nature [guṇa]. How then can it, being already in the jaws of the serpent, protect others?

verses.sanskrit: अहस्तानि सहस्तानामपदानि चतुष्पदाम् ।फल्गूनि तत्र महतां जीवो जीवस्य जीवनम् ॥ ४७ ॥
verses.transliteration: ahastāni sahastānāmapadāni catuṣ-padāmphalgūni tatra mahatāṁjīvo jīvasya jīvanam
verses.text: Those who are devoid of hands are prey for those who have hands; those devoid of legs are prey for the four-legged. The weak are the subsistence of the strong, and the general rule holds that one living being is food for another.

verses.sanskrit: तदिदं भगवान् राजन्नेक आत्मात्मनां स्वद‍ृक् ।अन्तरोऽनन्तरो भाति पश्य तं माययोरुधा ॥ ४८ ॥
verses.transliteration: tad idaṁ bhagavān rājanneka ātmātmanāṁ sva-dṛkantaro ’nantaro bhātipaśya taṁ māyayorudhā
verses.text: Therefore, O King, you should look to the Supreme Lord only, who is one without a second and who manifests Himself by different energies and is both within and without.

verses.sanskrit: सोऽयमद्य महाराज भगवान् भूतभावन: ।कालरूपोऽवतीर्णोऽस्यामभावाय सुरद्विषाम् ॥ ४९ ॥
verses.transliteration: so ’yam adya mahārājabhagavān bhūta-bhāvanaḥkāla-rūpo ’vatīrṇo ’syāmabhāvāya sura-dviṣām
verses.text: That Supreme Personality of Godhead, Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa, in the guise of all-devouring time [kāla-rūpa] has now descended to the earth to eliminate the envious from the world.

verses.sanskrit: निष्पादितं देवकृत्यमवशेषं प्रतीक्षते ।तावद् यूयमवेक्षध्वं भवेद् यावदिहेश्वर: ॥ ५० ॥
verses.transliteration: niṣpāditaṁ deva-kṛtyamavaśeṣaṁ pratīkṣatetāvad yūyam avekṣadhvaṁbhaved yāvad iheśvaraḥ
verses.text: The Lord has already performed His duties to help the demigods, and He is awaiting the rest. You Pāṇḍavas may wait as long as the Lord is here on earth.

verses.sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र: सह भ्रात्रा गान्धार्या च स्वभार्यया ।दक्षिणेन हिमवत ऋषीणामाश्रमं गत: ॥ ५१ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭraḥ saha bhrātrāgāndhāryā ca sva-bhāryayādakṣiṇena himavataṛṣīṇām āśramaṁ gataḥ
verses.text: O King, your uncle Dhṛtarāṣṭra, his brother Vidura and his wife Gāndhārī have gone to the southern side of the Himālaya Mountains, where there are shelters of the great sages.

verses.sanskrit: स्रोतोभि: सप्तभिर्या वै स्वर्धुनी सप्तधा व्यधात् ।सप्तानां प्रीतये नाना सप्तस्रोत: प्रचक्षते ॥ ५२ ॥
verses.transliteration: srotobhiḥ saptabhir yā vaisvardhunī saptadhā vyadhātsaptānāṁ prītaye nānāsapta-srotaḥ pracakṣate
verses.text: The place is called Saptasrota [“divided by seven”] because there the waters of the sacred Ganges were divided into seven branches. This was done for the satisfaction of the seven great ṛṣis.

verses.sanskrit: स्‍नात्वानुसवनं तस्मिन्हुत्वा चाग्नीन्यथाविधि ।अब्भक्ष उपशान्तात्मा स आस्ते विगतैषण: ॥ ५३ ॥
verses.transliteration: snātvānusavanaṁ tasminhutvā cāgnīn yathā-vidhiab-bhakṣa upaśāntātmāsa āste vigataiṣaṇaḥ
verses.text: On the banks at Saptasrota, Dhṛtarāṣṭra is now engaged in beginning aṣṭāṅga-yoga by bathing three times daily, in the morning, noon and evening, by performing the Agni-hotra sacrifice with fire and by drinking only water. This helps one control the mind and the senses and frees one completely from thoughts of familial affection.

verses.sanskrit: जितासनो जितश्वास: प्रत्याहृतषडिन्द्रिय: ।हरिभावनया ध्वस्तरज:सत्त्वतमोमल: ॥ ५४ ॥
verses.transliteration: jitāsano jita-śvāsaḥpratyāhṛta-ṣaḍ-indriyaḥhari-bhāvanayā dhvasta-rajaḥ-sattva-tamo-malaḥ
verses.text: One who has controlled the sitting postures [the yogic āsanas] and the breathing process can turn the senses toward the Absolute Personality of Godhead and thus become immune to the contaminations of the modes of material nature, namely mundane goodness, passion and ignorance.

verses.sanskrit: विज्ञानात्मनि संयोज्य क्षेत्रज्ञे प्रविलाप्य तम् ।ब्रह्मण्यात्मानमाधारे घटाम्बरमिवाम्बरे ॥ ५५ ॥
verses.transliteration: vijñānātmani saṁyojyakṣetrajñe pravilāpya tambrahmaṇy ātmānam ādhāreghaṭāmbaram ivāmbare
verses.text: Dhṛtarāṣṭra will have to amalgamate his pure identity with intelligence and then merge into the Supreme Being with knowledge of his qualitative oneness, as a living entity, with the Supreme Brahman. Being freed from the blocked sky, he will have to rise to the spiritual sky.

verses.sanskrit: ध्वस्तमायागुणोदर्को निरुद्धकरणाशय: ।निवर्तिताखिलाहार आस्ते स्थाणुरिवाचल: ।तस्यान्तरायो मैवाभू: सन्न्यस्ताखिलकर्मण: ॥ ५६ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhvasta-māyā-guṇodarkoniruddha-karaṇāśayaḥnivartitākhilāhāraāste sthāṇur ivācalaḥtasyāntarāyo maivābhūḥsannyastākhila-karmaṇaḥ
verses.text: He will have to suspend all the actions of the senses, even from the outside, and will have to be impervious to interactions of the senses, which are influenced by the modes of material nature. After renouncing all material duties, he must become immovably established, beyond all sources of hindrances on the path.

verses.sanskrit: स वा अद्यतनाद् राजन् परत: पञ्चमेऽहनि ।कलेवरं हास्यति स्वं तच्च भस्मीभविष्यति ॥ ५७ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa vā adyatanād rājanparataḥ pañcame ’hanikalevaraṁ hāsyati svaṁtac ca bhasmī-bhaviṣyati
verses.text: O King, he will quit his body, most probably on the fifth day from today. And his body will turn to ashes.

verses.sanskrit: दह्यमानेऽग्निभिर्देहे पत्यु: पत्नी सहोटजे ।बहि: स्थिता पतिं साध्वी तमग्निमनु वेक्ष्यति ॥ ५८ ॥
verses.transliteration: dahyamāne ’gnibhir dehepatyuḥ patnī sahoṭajebahiḥ sthitā patiṁ sādhvītam agnim anu vekṣyati
verses.text: While outside observing her husband, who will burn in the fire of mystic power along with his thatched cottage, his chaste wife will enter the fire with rapt attention.

verses.sanskrit: विदुरस्तु तदाश्चर्यं निशाम्य कुरुनन्दन ।हर्षशोकयुतस्तस्माद् गन्ता तीर्थनिषेवक: ॥ ५९ ॥
verses.transliteration: viduras tu tad āścaryaṁniśāmya kuru-nandanaharṣa-śoka-yutas tasmādgantā tīrtha-niṣevakaḥ
verses.text: Vidura, being affected with delight and grief, will then leave that place of sacred pilgrimage.

verses.sanskrit: इत्युक्त्वाथारुहत् स्वर्गं नारद: सहतुम्बुरु: ।युधिष्ठिरो वचस्तस्य हृदि कृत्वाजहाच्छुच: ॥ ६० ॥
verses.transliteration: ity uktvāthāruhat svargaṁnāradaḥ saha-tumburuḥyudhiṣṭhiro vacas tasyahṛdi kṛtvājahāc chucaḥ
verses.text: Having spoken thus, the great sage Nārada, along with his vīṇā, ascended into outer space. Yudhiṣṭhira kept his instruction in his heart and so was able to get rid of all lamentations.
